content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are two friends. Initially, the skill levels of them are zero. They work on alternative days, i.e one of Alice and Bob works on the odd-numbered days(1,3,5,… )(1, 3, 5, \dots)(1,3,5,…) and the other works on the even-numbered days (2,4,6,… )(2, 4, 6, \dots)(2,4,6,…). The skill levels of Alice and Bob increase by A,BA, BA,B respectively on the days they work.

Determine if it is possible that the skill levels of Alice and Bob become exactly P,QP, QP,Q respectively on some day.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow:
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers A,B,P,QA, B, P, QA,B,P,Q.


Output Format
For each test case, print `YES` if it is possible that the skill levels of Alice and Bob become exactly P,QP, QP,Q on some day, otherwise print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers A, B, P, Q.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible that after some day the skill levels of Alice and Bob are exactly P and Q respectively, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Alice works on day 1 (gain 1) and Bob on day 2 (gain 2) → (1,2). Test case 2: No combination yields (3,2). Test case 3: Bob works days 1 and 3 (2*3=6) and Alice works day 2 (1*4=4) → (4,6). Test case 4: Impossible to reach (9,25).
First case: a = 6/2 = 3, b = 9/3 = 3, diff = 0 → YES. Second case: a = 20/5 = 4, b = 14/7 = 2, diff = 2 → NO.

solution.approach: Let a = P / A and b = Q / B. The values must be integers, otherwise the required skill cannot be reached. Because Alice and Bob work on alternating days, the numbers of days each of them works can differ by at most one. Therefore the condition for existence is |a - b| <= 1. We check divisibility and the absolute difference for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
